commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=42574d819c2e7f917411026cc01875522173fc2b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes HTML export rendering to escape `entry.id` for message element IDs and copy-link `data-entry-id` attributes.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows raw `entry.id` interpolation into exported HTML attributes, so a crafted quote-bearing entry id can break the attribute boundary.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer handling for the proof gate plus a small code correction; this should not go to a repair lane while real behavior proof is still missing.

target.title: fix(security): escape entry.id in HTML export to prevent attribute XSS
target.body:
## Problem

`renderEntry` and `renderCopyLinkButton` in `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js` interpolate `entry.id` directly into `id="..."` and `data-entry-id="..."` HTML attributes without escaping. A crafted entry ID like `"><script>alert(1)</script>` causes attribute breakout and XSS in exported HTML files.

## Fix

Route `entry.id` through `escapeHtmlAttr()` at the two HTML insertion boundaries:

1. **`renderEntry`** (line 1323): `entry-${escapeHtmlAttr(entry.id)}` for the element `id` attribute
2. **`renderCopyLinkButton`** (line 1312): `escapeHtmlAttr(entryId)` for the `data-entry-id` attribute

The raw `entry.id` is passed to `renderCopyLinkButton` as a parameter (not pre-escaped), so escaping happens exactly once inside the function. Browsers decode HTML entities in `dataset` reads, so `dataset.entryId` returns the original raw ID for `buildShareUrl()`.

Production diff is +2 lines changed across 1 file. No CHANGELOG changes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Crafted `entry.id` values with `"`, `<`, `>` characters could break out of HTML attributes in exported session files. The `escapeHtmlAttr()` guard prevents attribute breakout and script injection while preserving copy-link round-trip correctness.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js v26.0.0, linkedom DOM parser, Vitest v4.1.7, run from worktree `/tmp/fix-83104` branched from current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node scripts/xss-dom-proof.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.security.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output and HTML artifact below.

### Real HTML export artifact proof

Generated a real HTML export file with malicious `entry.id = '"><script>alert(1)</script><div data-x="'` and inspected the rendered DOM via linkedom:

```console
$ node scripts/xss-dom-proof.mjs

=== DOM-Level XSS Proof ===

1. <script> tags in messages area: 0
   PASS: No script injection
2. Elements with onmouseover: none
   PASS: No attribute breakout
3. Copy-link button data-entry-id attribute: "\"><script>alert(1)</script><div data-x=\""
   dataset.entryId (browser-decoded): "\"><script>alert(1)</script><div data-x=\""
   Has stray data-x attribute: false
   PASS: No attribute leak
4. User message id attribute: "entry-\"><script>alert(1)</script><div data-x=\""
   Starts with "entry-": true
   Has stray data-x: false
   PASS: ID properly escaped
5. Special-char entry found: id="entry-msg-with\"quotes&amp's"
   PASS: Round-trip works for quotes and ampersands

6. Rendered DOM saved to /tmp/xss-proof-rendered-dom.html (1360 bytes)
```

### Raw HTML artifact inspection

The rendered DOM HTML shows the malicious payload is contained within attribute values via `"` entity escaping. No `<script>` element is injected as a DOM node:

```html
<div class="user-message" id="entry-"><script>alert(1)</script><div data-x="">
  <button class="copy-link-btn" data-entry-id=""><script>alert(1)</script><div data-x="" ...>
```

The `"` before `>` keeps the `>` inside the attribute value. The browser renders `<script>` as literal text within the attribute, not as an executable element. Check 1 confirms 0 `<script>` elements exist in the DOM.

### Copy-link round-trip proof

Entry ID with special characters `msg-with"quotes&amp's`:
- `escapeHtmlAttr()` encodes `"` as `"` in the `data-entry-id` attribute
- Browser `dataset.entryId` decodes `"` back to `"`, returning the raw ID
- `buildShareUrl()` receives the correct raw ID
- `document.getElementById("entry-msg-with\"quotes&amp's")` finds the element

### Vitest test suite (10 tests)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.security.test.ts
 ✓ |auto-reply| template.security.test.ts (10 tests) 135ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Observed result after fix: All 5 DOM checks pass. The crafted `entry.id` is entity-escaped in HTML attributes, preventing script injection and attribute breakout. Copy-link IDs round-trip correctly through browser HTML entity decoding.

What was not tested: Opening the exported HTML in a real browser. The DOM-level proof uses linkedom (a spec-compliant HTML parser) which matches browser behavior for attribute escaping and `dataset` decoding.
target.author: sebtardif

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
